project_description: Modify and lower selected McCormack-Williamson Tract levees and restore former agricultural land to subtidal, tidal marsh, and riparian habitats, improving floodplain connectivity, aquatic and terrestrial habitat, and North Delta flood resilience.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Delta smelt

project_description: Breach the perimeter levee, realign and extend tidal channels, and add a low drainage berm at Grizzly Slough, restoring tidal and fluvial connections and creating additional floodplain and wetland habitat for native fish and wildlife.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Delta smelt

project_description: Set back approximately seven miles of levees, remove portions of the existing levee system, and modify related flood facilities to widen the Yolo and Sacramento Bypasses, increasing flood conveyance and public safety while expanding inundated floodplain and riparian habitat.
project_type: bypass floodplain habitat; fish passage improvement
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Green sturgeon; White sturgeon

project_description: Remove rock revetment, recontour the riverbank, and convert agricultural land to native riparian and floodplain habitat along Kopta Slough, improving fish and wildlife habitat, river function, public recreation, and flood management efficiency.

Improve hydrologic connections between the Feather River, Sacramento River, and adjacent floodplain areas to create and enhance seasonal floodplain habitat that supports Chinook salmon and steelhead rearing.

Rehabilitate Tisdale Weir and install an operable fish passage facility connecting the weir notch to the Sacramento River, addressing structural deficiencies while reducing fish stranding and improving upstream passage for salmon, sturgeon, and other native fish.

Remove the existing earthen Agricultural Road Crossing #4 and replace it with a bridge spanning Tule Canal, restoring unimpeded fish movement while retaining agricultural and vehicle access during non-flood conditions.
funding_sources: Wildlife Conservation Board; United States Bureau of Reclamation
project_type: fish screen installation or improvement; fish passage improvement
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Green sturgeon; White sturgeon

project_name: Prospect Island Tidal Habitat Restoration Project
project_description: Restore tidal processes on Prospect Island by grading interior areas, managing vegetation, constructing channels and topographic features, and breaching exterior levees, converting about 1,600 acres to tidal habitat that supports Delta fish and improves food web productivity, water quality, carbon sequestration, and climate resilience.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Delta smelt

project_description: Transform the Little Egbert Tract into flood conveyance and habitat zones by modifying levees and infrastructure; creating inflow and outflow connections; constructing subtidal swales and habitat berms; and restoring native riparian, tidal, subtidal, and floodplain vegetation to improve flood protection, native fish habitat, and recreation.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Green sturgeon; White sturgeon

project_description: Restore and enhance roughly 2,200 acres at the downstream end of the Yolo Bypass by creating connected tidal marsh, floodplain, wetland, and riparian habitat while retaining compatible wildlife-friendly agriculture and managed wetlands, improving habitat connectivity, flood capacity, drainage, and irrigation.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Delta smelt

project_description: Convert former grazing and dairy land near Oakley to tidal marsh by grading the remaining project parcel, restoring native vegetation, and breaching levees to reestablish daily tidal exchange, expanding habitat for native Delta fish and wildlife while improving flood protection, public access, education, and recreation.
target_species: Chinook salmon; Steelhead trout; Delta smelt

project_name: Feather River Salmonid Spawning Habitat Improvement (2024)
project_description: Place, sort, and harvest appropriately sized gravel and cobble in the Feather River to rebuild spawning substrate, increasing the quantity and quality of accessible spawning habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: General Fund; Local Assistance

project_name: Feather River Salmonid Spawning Habitat Improvement (Proposition 68)
project_description: Place and distribute spawning-sized gravel at targeted Feather River locations to expand accessible spawning habitat for salmonids, building on the first-phase gravel additions and supporting continued Chinook salmon and steelhead reproduction.
funding_sources: Proposition 68; United States Bureau of Reclamation; California Department of Water Resources

project_name: Mokelumne River Day Use Area Large Floodplain Project
project_description: Reconnect and lower floodplain surfaces in the Mokelumne River Day Use Area to create seasonally inundated habitat, increasing rearing opportunities for juvenile Chinook salmon and restoring floodplain function.

project_name: Mokelumne River Gravel Maintenance Project (Early Implementation)
project_description: Maintain spawning gravel at suitable elevations in the Lower Mokelumne River through targeted gravel replenishment and management, sustaining accessible spawning habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead where dams and historic mining have reduced natural gravel supply.
funding_sources: California Department of Water Resources; East Bay Municipal Utility District

project_name: Mokelumne River Floodplain Restoration Project (Early Implementation)
project_description: Restore and reconnect floodplain areas in the Mokelumne River Day Use Area to provide seasonal inundation and shallow-water rearing habitat for juvenile Chinook salmon while improving floodplain processes.
funding_sources: Proposition 68; East Bay Municipal Utility District

project_name: Mokelumne River Conservation Easement Large Floodplain Restoration Project
project_description: Reconnect and restore a larger Mokelumne River floodplain area to provide seasonally inundated juvenile salmon rearing habitat and improve floodplain function.
funding_sources: California Department of Water Resources; East Bay Municipal Utility District

project_name: Mokelumne River In-Channel Rearing Habitat Restoration Project
project_description: Implement in-channel habitat improvements on the Lower Mokelumne River to create shallow, hydraulically diverse rearing conditions for juvenile Chinook salmon and improve the ecological function of the regulated river reach.
funding_sources: California Department of Water Resources; East Bay Municipal Utility District

project_description: Continue targeted maintenance of Lower Mokelumne River spawning gravel so suitable substrate remains available for Chinook salmon and steelhead despite limited natural gravel recruitment below upstream dams.
funding_sources: California Department of Water Resources; East Bay Municipal Utility District

project_name: Oroville Wildlife Area River Restoration Project at Robinson's Riffle
project_description: Excavate relict mine tailings, construct gravel bars and riffles, reconnect the Feather River to its historic floodplain, and create seasonal side-channel, pool, and riparian features at Robinson's Riffle, improving Chinook salmon and steelhead spawning and rearing habitat while reducing flood stages and supporting recreation.
funding_sources: Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund
target_species: Chinook salmon; Pacific lamprey; White sturgeon; Green sturgeon; Steelhead trout

project_description: Restore approximately one-half mile of Putah Creek by narrowing and meandering the low-flow channel and lowering the floodplain, increasing the extent and frequency of inundation to improve juvenile Chinook salmon rearing habitat and habitat for other native fish.
contractors: Harris and Assoc; NHC; Verdantas; Wood Rogers
funding_sources: Proposition 68

project_description: Periodically add and scarify gravel in Putah Creek to maintain clean, suitably sized spawning substrate, improving Chinook salmon spawning habitat in a reach with limited natural sediment replenishment.
funding_sources: Solano County Water Agency

project_name: Putah Creek fish passage at Los Rios Check Dam
project_description: Advance planning, design, environmental review, and permitting for fish-passage improvements at Los Rios Check Dam so Chinook salmon can move more freely through Putah Creek and reach upstream habitat.
funding_sources: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

project_name: Putah Creek fish passage at County Rd 106a
project_description: Advance planning, design, environmental review, and permitting for fish passage improvements at the County Road 106A crossing, reducing an obstruction to Chinook salmon movement and improving access to habitat in Putah Creek.
funding_sources: Wildlife Conservation Board

project_name: Upstream of Old LaGrange Bridge Restoration Project
project_description: Restore about one mile of the Tuolumne River upstream of Old La Grange Bridge by reconstructing and contouring riffles, placing coarse sediment and spawning gravel, and reconnecting adjacent floodplain surfaces, increasing channel complexity and spawning and rearing habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
lead_entity: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission; Modesto Irrigation District; Turlock Irrigation District
funding_sources: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission; Merced Irrigation District; Turlock Irrigation District; California Department of Fish and Wildlife
project_type: fish food production; rearing habitat; spawning habitat; tributary floodplain habitat

project_description: Rebuild approximately 2,500 feet of Lower Tuolumne River habitat below La Grange Dam by adding clean gravel and constructing riffles, gravel bars, habitat boulders, and large wood features, replacing simplified cascades and pools with more complex spawning and rearing habitat for salmonids.
lead_entity: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission; Modesto Irrigation District; Turlock Irrigation District
funding_sources: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission; Merced Irrigation District; Turlock Irrigation District
project_type: fish food production; rearing habitat; spawning habitat; tributary floodplain habitat

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat in the Lower American River at Nimbus Basin by replenishing and sorting clean gravel, carving and enhancing side channels, adding large wood, and establishing riparian vegetation to provide suitable spawning substrate, cover, shade, and food-web habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: Proposition 68; Water Forum

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat at Upper Sailor Bar by replenishing and sorting clean gravel and enhancing side-channel and floodplain features, increasing suitable substrate, cover, and shallow-water habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: United States Bureau of Reclamation; Water Forum

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat at Lower Sailor Bar by replenishing and sorting clean gravel, carving and enhancing side channels, adding large wood, and establishing riparian vegetation to provide spawning substrate, cover, shade, and food web habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: Proposition 68; Water Forum

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat at Ancil Hoffman Park by replenishing and sorting clean gravel and enhancing side-channel and floodplain features, increasing suitable substrate and protected shallow-water habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: United States Bureau of Reclamation; Water Forum

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat at Upper River Bend by replenishing and sorting clean gravel and enhancing side-channel and floodplain features, improving suitable substrate and protected shallow-water habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: Proposition 68; United States Bureau of Reclamation; Water Forum

project_description: Restore spawning and juvenile rearing habitat at River Bend by replenishing and sorting clean gravel and enhancing side-channel and floodplain features, improving suitable substrate and protected shallow-water habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead.
funding_sources: United States Bureau of Reclamation; Water Forum

project_name: Hallwood Side Channel and Floodplain Restoration Project
project_description: Create and enhance frequently inundated floodplain, side-channel, and other off-channel habitat along the lower Yuba River, increasing protected seasonal rearing habitat for Chinook salmon and steelhead while reducing local flood-stage elevations and velocities.
contractors: Verdantas; Cramer Fish Sciences; South Yuba River Citizen League; Teichert
funding_sources: United States Bureau of Reclamation; Yuba Water Agency; Proposition 1; Proposition 68

project_name: Lower Long Bar Habitat Enhancement Project
project_description: Lower and contour floodplain surfaces at Lower Long Bar to increase seasonal inundation and create rearing habitat for juvenile Chinook salmon and steelhead, improving floodplain connectivity and habitat diversity along the lower Yuba River.
contractors: Verdantas; Cramer Fish Sciences; Blankinship & Associates, a Bowman Company; Taber Construction
funding_sources: United States Fish and Wildlife Service

project_name: Upper Long Bar Habitat Enhancement Project
project_description: Lower and contour floodplain surfaces at Upper Long Bar to increase seasonal inundation and create rearing habitat for juvenile Chinook salmon and steelhead, improving floodplain connectivity and habitat diversity along the lower Yuba River.
contractors: Verdantas; Cramer Fish Sciences; Anderson Dragline Construction
funding_sources: Yuba Water Agency; Wildlife Conservation Board; California Department of Fish and Wildlife

project_name: Upper Rose Bar Habitat Enhancement Project
project_description: Place spawning-sized gravel to construct two riffles, excavate a side-channel floodplain area, and create a lateral rearing feature at Upper Rose Bar, expanding Chinook salmon and steelhead spawning and juvenile rearing habitat.
contractors: Verdantas; Cramer Fish Sciences; Blankinship & Associates, a Bowman Company; Hansen Brothers Construction
funding_sources: Wildlife Conservation Board; Yuba Water Agency
